response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack and web developer with broad language exposure, but is a poor fit for this embedded networking security role. The position requires native-level Japanese, strong C for UNIX development, Linux kernel source analysis, and hands-on experience with L2/L3 switching and security technologies like MACsec and 802.1X — none of which are evidenced. C/C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments, and there is no embedded firmware, networking protocol, or security-feature work in the history.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some systems work
Strong engineering craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing) and open-source contributions
Some low-level/performance experience (DLang low-latency services, C/C++ at National Instruments, Linux)
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — a hard requirement (native-level) for this role
No embedded firmware, LAN-switch, L2/L3 networking, or security technology (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption) experience
Minimal recent C programming and no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience
